data.title: Astec Industries, Inc. Announces the Company’s First Quarter 2026 Conference Call on May 6, 2026, at 8:30 A.M. Eastern Time
data.description: A company based in Chattanooga, Tennessee, Astec Industries, Inc. (NASDAQ: ASTE) has scheduled a conference call on April 15, 2026, to discuss its financial performance for the first quarter of the year. Industry participants and investors are welcomed to join this review of the company's financial results.

data.description: Arcutis unveiled today that it is set to release its financial results for the first quarter of 2026 and deliver a business update on May 6, 2026, following the closing of the U.S. markets.

data.title: Expand Energy Provides 2026 First Quarter Earnings Conference Call Information
data.description: A press release from Expand Energy Corporation (NASDAQ: EXE) based in Spring, Texas, on April 15, 2026, stated that the company will disclose its first quarter results for 2026 after the market closes on April 28, 2026. The company has organized a conference call to review these results which will take place on April 29, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. EDT. Interested participants can access the live webcast by visiting the provided link. To ask a question during the conference call, participants must register in advance to receive the necessary dial-in information and a unique PIN code for access. The links for the conference call will be shared on Expand Energy’s official website. A replay of the call will also be accessible on the website post the event.

data.title: Revenue Optics Completes Full Commercial Buildout. A Nine-Month-Old Firm Built on 25 Years of Distribution Expertise. Five Clients From $200M to $3B
data.description: Revenue Optics has recently made four key leadership appointments. All three business lines are fully staffed and operational. This has resulted in the establishment of an enterprise-ready company focused on B2B distribution exclusively. The firm, based in Memphis, Tennessee, completed its commercial leadership team expansion on April 15, 2026.

data.title: FertilAI Secures World's First CE Mark for Predictive AI in Fertility Treatment Cycle Optimization
data.description: FertilAI's StimAI and OvuPredict have recently obtained clearance under the EU Medical Device Regulation (MDR), granting fertility clinics the capability to forecast the most suitable timing for treatment, improve clinical accuracy, and manage workloads more effectively. The announcement was made in Tel Aviv, Israel, on April 15, 2026. This achievement enables FertilAI to enhance their services within the fertility sector, providing advanced technology solutions.

data.title: Cerebras Systems Closes $850 Million Revolving Credit Facility
data.description: Cerebras Systems, based in Sunnyvale, California, a company known for producing the quickest AI infrastructure, has recently finalized a new syndicated revolving credit facility lasting for five years, with a maximum amount of $850 million. This development comes subsequent to the company's successful closure of a $1 billion Series G financing in September 2025 and an additional $1 billion Series H in January 2026. Expressing contentment, Bob Komi stated, "We are delighted to have concluded our first credit facility with the backing of prominent financial institutions."

data.title: Wisconsin Supreme Court Sides with Family in Asbestos Case Against Pabst Brewing Co.
data.description: In Milwaukee, the Wisconsin Supreme Court has upheld a ruling holding Pabst Brewing Co. accountable for asbestos exposure at its brewery. This decision stems from a prolonged legal battle initiated by the relatives of a steamfitter, Jerry Lorbiecki, who succumbed to mesothelioma. Jerry had encountered asbestos during his tenure at the brewery in the 1970s under an external contractor. Following a jury's verdict, Pabst has been determined liable for the incident, underscoring the ongoing legal challenges in the case.

data.title: Oklo, NuScale Stocks Rally On US Space Nuclear Power Initiative
data.description: Shares of innovative nuclear technology companies Oklo Inc. (NYSE: OKLO) and NuScale Power Corp. (NYSE: SMR) saw significant increases in value on Wednesday in response to the U.S. government's announcement of a comprehensive program to advance nuclear energy technology in space. The initiative, known as the National Initiative for American Space Nuclear Power (NSTM-3), is a collaborative effort between NASA and the Pentagon aiming to develop nuclear power systems capable of supporting upcoming lunar and interplanetary missions. As part of this initiative, a 20-kilowatt electric (kWe) space reactor named "SR-1 Freedom" is being designed for interplanetary propulsion and surface power. The program includes plans for an in-orbit demonstration by 2028 and the installation of a functional reactor for space use. This groundbreaking development has garnered official endorsement from NASA and is set to revolutionize nuclear power in space exploration. For more details, visit Benzinga.com for the complete story.

data.title: Lautenbach Recycling to Celebrate Earth Day with Grand Opening of Bellingham Facility
data.description: The city of Bellingham in Washington will witness a significant event on April 22nd, 2026, as Lautenbach Recycling plans to commemorate Earth Day with a ribbon-cutting ceremony at 3:30 p.m. Following this, a community open house will be held from 4:00 p.m. to 6:00 p.m. The celebration will take place at the new facility located at 2885 E. Bakerview Road. This event signifies a crucial step in promoting environmental sustainability and community engagement.

data.title: /C O R R E C T I O N -- Service Compression/
data.description: Service Compression has recently completed the acquisition of Axip Energy Services, as announced in a news release dated April 15, 2026. The acquisition was disclosed by Service Compression through PR Newswire. Following this acquisition, significant changes have been implemented by the company. Additional details regarding this acquisition can be found at the end of the revised release.

data.title: Contractor Forced to Pay $200,000 in Back Pay For Violating St. Louis County Prevailing Wage Law
data.description: In connection with the investigation, the Mid-America Carpenters Regional Council collaborated on the case in St. Louis. Recently, it was revealed that a contractor involved in the restoration of the Creve Coeur Park Soccer Complex project was obligated to compensate approximately $200,000 in back wages. This incident took place in St. Louis County on April 15, 2026.
